definition: The severity of market cascades is determined more by the extremity of pre-existing positioning than by the magnitude of the triggering event. Current positioning reads across key markets are elevated-to-extreme.
evidence: Historical cascade analysis showing positioning as dominant severity factor
Current positioning indicators at elevated-to-extreme levels
Trigger independence observed across multiple historical episodes
open_questions: What specific positioning metrics best predict cascade severity?
At what threshold does positioning become self-correcting vs. self-reinforcing?
How does private credit opacity distort positioning measurement?
